title: Crispy Canadian Chicken Skillet
description: A comforting, nut‑free skillet dish featuring seared chicken thighs, a grape‑juice glaze, and a crisp coating for a quick sick‑day dinner.
instructions: Pat the chicken pieces dry and season with salt and pepper.
Lightly dust the chicken in flour, shaking off excess.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat; add chicken and brown on all sides, about 6 minutes. Transfer chicken to a plate.
Reduce heat to medium, add butter to the skillet, then sauté onions until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Add garlic and thyme, cook 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in tomato paste, then pour in grape juice and chicken broth, scraping up browned bits.
Return chicken to the skillet, reduce heat to medium‑low, cover, and simmer until chicken is cooked through (internal temp 165°F), about 8‑10 minutes.
Increase heat to high, let the sauce reduce and thicken slightly, about 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper, then serve hot, optionally garnished with chopped fresh herbs.
